content:
Example name:
STORY-RECURSION-INDEX-ARC-0203-v1_1

1.6 WORKFLOW Layer

Function: defines concrete procedures, checklists, and runbooks.

Metaformeme: standard phrases are used to describe recurring tasks, such as “seed”, “fan”, “fold”, “seal.” Each has a specific operational meaning and associated expectations for artifacts.

Myth Fusion: workflows are conceived as rituals belonging to certain orders or cults (for example, “Rite of Seeding a World,” “Rite of Archive Consolidation”). This supports memorable reuse and layered symbolism.

Wave Theory: workflows are tuned to wave phases. For instance, in ramp-up phases, workflows prioritize breadth and exploration; during crest, they prioritize integration and release; during decay, they prioritize refactoring and archiving.

Recursive Storytelling: every workflow spec includes a short narrative example showing how it played out in an actual or hypothetical session, making it easier for future AIs or humans to internalize.

Example name:
